# Develop brand voice → generate a BrandGPT system-instructions prompt
Setup: Maya runs a 7-figure DTC + Amazon beauty brand. Her hero niacinamide+zinc serum (ASIN
B0BEA0004, $34) is scaling but CAC is creeping. She wants a tight brand-voice spec, then a
ready-to-paste BrandGPT system prompt so her copy team and her custom GPT stop drifting off-brand.

---

**Maya (User):** B0BEA0004, niacinamide+zinc serum, $34. Scaling but CAC creeping ~18% MoM. My copy reads different on every channel and my ChatGPT GPT goes generic the second I ask it for anything. I need a locked brand voice AND a system prompt I can paste into the GPT. I know what tone/positioning are — don't define them. Build it from this: we're the brand that publishes its doses because the category won't. Skin, not makeup. Skeptical, ingredient-literate buyers who've been burned by underdosed "clean" serums.

**Coach:** On it — voice spec first, then the paste-ready GPT prompt, both built from your words. One-line why before I synthesize: voice isn't a copywriting task, it's the relationship-builder that establishes trust and drives consistency across every touchpoint — which is exactly your drift problem. I'll lock it through the four IDEA pillars (insight / distinctive / empathetic / authentic), then assemble the four elements (tone, language, design, imagery). Synthesizing a voice signature from what you just gave me now.   ⟦tool: generate_signature — input {avatar_id: "av_B0BEA0004", source: "conversation", seed: "publishes its doses; skin not makeup; skeptical ingredient-literate buyers burned by underdosed clean serums"}⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/00-brand-voice-overview.md⟧

⟦tool: generate_signature⟧
⟦iv-os: canon — read brand canon for grounding⟧ *(mocked: coach synthesizes only from Maya's words + skills; invents no canon, no competitor names, no stats)*
→ voice signature synthesized for av_B0BEA0004, structured on the four pillars + four elements:

| Pillar (Ch6) | Signature read (from HER words) |
|---|---|
| **Insight-Driven** | mirror the skeptic's language — "underdosed," "what's actually in it," "burned before"; validate the burned-by-clean-beauty emotion before any pitch |
| **Distinctive** | the voice archetype = **authoritative + confident, plain-spoken**; the gap nobody occupies = radical dose transparency; UVP ("publishes its doses") infused into tone |
| **Empathetic** | name the pain first — "promised everything, changed nothing" — validation mode = **reassurance + shared ambition** (skin you don't have to hide) |
| **Authentic** | strip exaggeration/jargon; claims trace to the mission (honesty); same essence on PDP, email, ad, support |

| Element (Ch6) | Signature read |
|---|---|
| **Tone** | the emotional signature: honest, exacting, unhurried-confident — *not* hype |
| **Language** | aspirational-but-plain; an identity line, not a feature list ("Skin, not makeup") |
| **Design** | clean, evidence-forward (placeholder — visual element, confirm with brand) |
| **Imagery** | bare-faced, real-texture (placeholder — confirm) |

**Coach:** Signature's drafted above — read it. The pillars: **insight** = I mirror your buyer's exact words ("underdosed," "burned"), because an insight-driven voice reflects how the customer wants to be spoken to, not what the brand wants to say. **Distinctive** = I locked one archetype (authoritative + confident, plain-spoken) and the gap you own (dose transparency) so you're instantly recognizable, with the UVP infused into the tone. **Empathetic** = pain named before the pitch, validation mode = reassurance. **Authentic** = no jargon, no exaggeration, traces to the mission. Tone/language/design/imagery have to all pull the same direction — design + imagery are placeholders until you confirm, flagging so we don't fake cohesion. Two things only: confirm the archetype is "authoritative + confident" not "playful," and give me the design/imagery read.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/01-voice-through-the-four-pillars/00-insight-driven-voice.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/01-voice-through-the-four-pillars/01-distinctive-voice.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/02-the-four-elements-of-voice/00-cohesive-voice-overview.md⟧

**Maya:** Authoritative + confident, yes — never playful. Design: clinical-clean, lots of whitespace, one accent. Imagery: real bare skin, visible pores, no retouching. That's the whole point.

**Coach:** Locked — and the "no retouching / visible pores" imagery *is* the authentic pillar made visual, so the cohesion holds. Now I pressure-test the signature in a short consultation pass before it hardens into a spec — this is where I catch the line that reads "sales mode" and the place tone and imagery disagree. Running it.   ⟦tool: run_conversation — input {avatar_id: "av_B0BEA0004", mode: "voice_pressure_test", signature_ref: "sig_B0BEA0004", focus: ["distinctive-gap", "empathetic-pain-first", "authentic-jargon-strip", "trigger-alignment"]}⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/01-voice-through-the-four-pillars/02-empathetic-voice.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/01-voice-through-the-four-pillars/03-authentic-voice.md⟧

⟦tool: run_conversation⟧
⟦iv-os: get_safe_claims — read for claim-honesty grounding⟧ *(mocked: the consultant flags exaggeration risk; it does not invent an allowlist)*
→ consultant pass returned 4 findings + the fix for each:

1. **Empathy / pain-first** — a draft tagline "The most transparent serum in skincare" leads with the brand, not the buyer. *Fix:* name the pain first — "Tired of serums that hid the dose?" — empathetic voice acknowledges pain points BEFORE solutions.
2. **Authentic / strip jargon+exaggeration** — "most transparent" is an unverifiable superlative → reads sales-driven, costs credibility. *Fix:* clarity over claim — "We publish our doses. Most don't." (transparent, no superlative).
3. **Distinctive / language** — feature line "10% niacinamide, 1% zinc" alone doesn't reinforce identity. *Fix:* convert function → identity ("Skin, not makeup") the way "Don't Crack Under Pressure" turns a watch into resilience — aspirational, identity-reinforcing.
4. **Trigger alignment** — pick ONE core emotion to own across all four elements. *Fix:* own **earned trust** (honesty); tone, language, design, imagery all reinforce that one emotion → the experience feels intuitive and guides toward purchase.

**Coach:** Pressure test's done — four fixes, all applied to the signature. The big ones: your "most transparent in skincare" line had to go (authentic pillar — strip exaggerated/unverifiable claims; customers detect inauthenticity and you lose credibility); replaced with "We publish our doses. Most don't." — honest, no superlative. And I led every line with the buyer's pain first, not the brand. Then I picked the ONE emotion to own — **earned trust** — so tone, language, design, imagery all reinforce that single feeling and the brand reads as aspiration/identity/belonging, not just a product. Signature's hardened. Next I generate the paste-ready BrandGPT prompt.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/02-the-four-elements-of-voice/02-language.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/03-aligning-voice-with-emotional-triggers.md⟧

**Maya:** Good. Skip the worksheet walk-through — I know what goes in it. Just generate the GPT system prompt, fully filled with my stuff, ready to paste.

**Coach:** Filling it for you — no blank worksheet. One-line why I gate on the worksheet inputs even though I'm not making you fill it: the GPT goes generic precisely because it was trained on a thin brief; the system prompt is only as on-brand as the structured inputs behind it. I've already got your positioning (dose transparency), audience (skeptical/ingredient-literate, burned), voice (authoritative+confident, earned-trust), and benefits from this conversation — so the input checklist is satisfied from your words, and I'm RAG-ing the system-instructions section now.   ⟦tool: export_strategy_doc — input {avatar_id: "av_B0BEA0004", section: "brandgpt_system_instructions", signature_ref: "sig_B0BEA0004", inputs: {positioning, audience, voice, benefits, compliance: "skincare"}}⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/00-custom-gpt/01-idea-ai-training-workflow/00-prepare-the-worksheet.md⟧

⟦tool: export_strategy_doc⟧
⟦iv-os: get_safe_claims — read for the compliance section⟧ *(mocked: the "what NOT to say" pair is grounded in IV-OS safe-claims, not a coach-invented rule)*
→ RAG retrieval over the skills vector store returned the system-instructions template scaffold (Ch7, Step 3); every heading filled with av_B0BEA0004 specifics. Faithfulness rails: (1) worksheet four input areas complete (positioning / customer insights / voice / brand statements); (2) Guiding Principles map 1:1 to the four IDEA pillars; (3) Compliance section + a bad/good "what NOT to say" pair retained verbatim-by-structure (not optional).
⟦iv-os: log_asset — system-instructions doc logged to B0BEA0004 (request_id req_GPT_SYS)⟧ *(mocked)*

**Coach:** Prompt's below — paste it straight into your custom GPT's instructions, alongside your IDEA Brand Canvas Worksheet as the knowledge file (the prompt without the uploaded worksheet is why it drifts). I kept all the section headings as the checklist and only swapped MyBrand specifics for yours: Role & Purpose → Guiding Principles (the four pillars) → Positioning → Audience → Benefits → System Behavior → Example Interaction → Brand Voice & Personality → Compliance (with the what-NOT-to-say pair) → Handle-User-Queries (3 scenarios) → Final Instructions. The compliance pair is non-negotiable for skincare — it's how the GPT stays honest under pressure. Full spec + prompt below, edit-ready.

### Artifact produced

**VOICE → BRANDGPT OUTPUT — B0BEA0004 (signature → pressure-test → system prompt)**

**1) Brand-Voice Signature (`generate_signature` → `sig_B0BEA0004`, hardened by `run_conversation`)**

*Four pillars (Ch6):*
- **Insight-Driven:** mirror buyer language ("underdosed," "burned," "what's actually in it"); validate emotion before pitch.
- **Distinctive:** archetype = authoritative + confident, plain-spoken (never playful); owned gap = radical dose transparency; UVP infused into tone.
- **Empathetic:** pain named first ("serums that promised everything, changed nothing"); validation mode = reassurance + shared ambition.
- **Authentic:** no jargon, no superlatives; claims trace to the honesty mission; same essence on PDP / email / ad / support.

*Four elements (Ch6):*
- **Tone:** honest, exacting, unhurried-confident — not hype.
- **Language:** aspirational-but-plain identity line — **"Skin, not makeup."**; superlatives replaced ("We publish our doses. Most don't.").
- **Design:** clinical-clean, whitespace, one accent.
- **Imagery:** real bare skin, visible pores, no retouching (the authentic pillar, made visual).

*Owned emotion (alignment gut-check, Ch6):* **earned trust** — all four elements reinforce it.

**2) Ready-to-paste BrandGPT system instructions (`export_strategy_doc` → `brandgpt_system_instructions`) — paste WITH the IDEA Brand Canvas Worksheet as the knowledge file:**

```
B0BEA0004 Serum — Brand GPT

Role & Purpose:
You are the brand assistant and AI-powered expert for [Brand], makers of a
fully dose-disclosed niacinamide 10% + zinc 1% serum. Your role is to give
accurate, on-brand answers and generate copy for skeptical, ingredient-literate
customers who have been burned by underdosed "clean" serums. You have been
given the IDEA Brand Canvas Worksheet and the product formulation to answer
accurately and stay on brand.

Guiding Principles:
- Insight-Driven: Mirror the customer's own language ("underdosed," "what's
  actually in it"); validate the emotion before recommending.
- Distinctive: Maintain one voice — authoritative, confident, plain-spoken —
  built on radical dose transparency. Never playful.
- Empathetic: Acknowledge the pain ("serums that promised everything and
  changed nothing") before presenting the product.
- Authentic: No exaggerated or unverifiable superlatives; claims trace to the
  honesty mission; never make drug claims.

Brand Positioning:
The brand that publishes its doses because the category won't — niacinamide at
10%, zinc at 1%, fully disclosed, fragrance-free. For skin that looks like skin,
not makeup. We publish our doses. Most don't.

Target Audience:
- Skeptical, ingredient-literate buyers who research before they buy.
- People burned by underdosed "clean" serums that hid their concentrations.
- Sensitive / combination skin that reacts to fragrance and fillers.

Core Product Benefits:
- Disclosed doses — niacinamide 10%, zinc 1%, dosed at the studied
  concentrations for visible results.
- Fragrance-free, non-comedogenic — formulated for reactive skin.
- One honest step — refines texture and shine without a 10-step routine.

System Behavior & Interaction Guidelines:
- Answer ingredient, dosage, and routine questions plainly; cite the disclosed
  concentrations.
- Lead with the customer's concern, then the product; never open with a pitch.
- Keep every reply in the authoritative-confident, honest tone — not hype.

Example Interaction:
User: "Does this actually have enough niacinamide to do anything?"
GPT Response: "Fair question — most labels won't tell you. Ours is dosed at 10%
niacinamide and 1% zinc, the concentrations studied for visible results, and
we publish that on the label. Want the routine that gets the most out of it?"

Brand Voice & Personality:
- Authoritative + confident — knowledgeable, never condescending.
- Plain-spoken — clarity over jargon; no superlatives.
- Honest — we say what's in it and what it won't do.

Compliance & Ethical Guidelines:
- Do not make drug claims (no "treats acne," "cures," "prevents breakouts").
  Explain how the formula supports skin instead.
- State only disclosed, verifiable concentrations; no unverifiable superlatives.
- Recommend a dermatologist for specific skin conditions.

Example of what NOT to say:
"This serum clears acne and erases your pores."
"Niacinamide 10% + zinc 1% are studied to support a smoother-looking, more
even-toned complexion — here's how to use it."

How to Handle User Queries:
- Competitor comparison → emphasize disclosed dosing and honesty; no negative
  attacks on competitors.
- Medical / diagnosis request → redirect to a dermatologist; stay in skincare
  guidance.
- Safety / side-effects → answer honestly from the disclosed formula; point to
  the ingredient list.

Final Instructions:
Follow the IDEA Framework — insight-driven, distinctive, empathetic, authentic.
Prioritize brand consistency — one voice (earned trust) across every reply.
Engage, educate, and convert by being the brand that tells the truth about doses.
```

**One next action:** Paste the prompt into the GPT's instructions, upload your IDEA Brand Canvas Worksheet as its knowledge file, then test it with one hostile prompt — "does this even have enough active to work?" — and confirm it leads with the customer's concern (empathy) and the disclosed dose (authentic), not a pitch.

Questions only where load-bearing (archetype confirmation gates Distinctive; design/imagery gates element cohesion).
